tagline: From a proven ETH retreat to year-round builder infrastructure in Florianópolis.
summary: ETHFloripa Community Meetups proposes a credible, year-long upgrade from a proven high-signal ETH retreat (ETH Floripa 2025) into consistent local meetup infrastructure in Florianópolis. The application combines strong place-based grounding (Floripa’s tech ecosystem and existing ETH community), a clear builder-first roadmap with well-scoped themes (from protocol development and security to governance and public goods), and strong execution readiness (experienced team, partner networks, and low/zero-cost venue access through universities and technology parks). This Acceptance Summary highlights the strongest fit signals, includes Ethereum Everywhere reviewer notes, and notes a few optional recommendations to strengthen explicit localism experiments and learning value over the year.

standouts.lead: Proven execution and local demand signal (not a “new meetup idea” with no history)
standouts.body: grounded in a successful flagship event and an active community base: “Co-organized ETH Floripa 2025 (168 participants, 48 speakers, 16 partner communities)” and a “248-member Telegram group.”

standouts.lead: Well-scoped, infrastructure-aware roadmap with strong technical themes
standouts.body: indicating the series is designed for depth and builder outcomes (not generic Web3 talks): “Protocol development, L2 scaling, ZK research,” “Smart contract security and auditing practices,” and “Governance mechanisms and DAO tooling.”

standouts.lead: Strong network effects and ecosystem connectivity
standouts.body: with explicit ties to both local institutions and broader Ethereum networks: “16 partner communities including ETH Latam, Devconnect…,” plus “Protocol partnerships… Uniswap, Base, Octant, Chainlink, Fuel, Juicebox.”

standouts.lead: High-quality experience orientation with a realistic, transparent budget and low-cost venue strategy
standouts.body: aligning with the round’s intent to fund recurring IRL consistency: “Total Annual Budget Requested (for 12 Meetups): $3,000 USD,” and venues “at an extremely low or zero cost” via “Universities and Technology Parks.”

standouts.lead: Clear intent to build durable local capacity (not one-off events)
standouts.body: through leadership pathways and community ownership mechanisms: “Leadership pathways — Encourage attendees to lead sessions…” and “Collective ownership — Make community members feel like stakeholders…”.

reviewerNotes: Good plan. It looks like all infrastructure needs are well accounted for in their roadmap.
The proposed core themes are very well thought out, and the team behind it seems fully capable of executing as planned.
Most of the requested financial support will go toward ensuring a high-quality experience.
alignmentStrengths: Place-based grounding & local ecosystem: a specific Florianópolis context and a clear local community nucleus with demonstrated activity.
Local leadership and execution capacity: an experienced core team and proof of event delivery.
Network effects & cosmo-localism: extensive community/protocol relationships that can bring high-signal knowledge into Floripa and export learnings outward.
Sustainability readiness: low/zero-cost venue strategy and a modest budget focused on attendee experience.
alignmentGaps: Political and economic localism are present mainly as topic areas (“civic infrastructure,” “stablecoins… local economic applications”), but concrete local pilots are not clearly specified.
Ecological localism is lightly referenced (e.g., “regenerative finance”), but no explicit ecological project or partnership is described.

payout.label: Initial paid
payout.detail: Initial $750 paid in the January 2026 onboarding batches, when all ten meetups received their first release. The receiving wallet isn't published on the project's Karma page, so the payment is attributed by batch count; one further quarterly release (16 May 2026) also belongs to one of the five meetups in this group.
